The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: a854e93b). ## Issue to Resolve [Bug] AnkiDroidJS API returns incorrect data type ###### Reproduction Steps 1. Add a new basic type note. 2. Add the following code in the front template of the note: ```html api.ankiGetCardQueue() return value type: {{Front}} ``` 3. Study the new card in AnkiDroid. ###### Expected Result According to the `AnkiDroid Javascript API wiki`, the return value type of api.ankiGetCardQueue() is [`int`](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/AnkiDroid-Javascript-API#type-of-return-value-17). Therefore, it should display `api.ankiGetCardQueue() return value type: int` on the front of the card. ###### Actual Result It displays `api.ankiGetCardQueue() return value type: string` in the front of the card. ###### Debug info ``` AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 10 (SDK 29) ProductFlavor = full Manufacturer = HUAWEI Model = MXW-AN00 Hardware = mt6873 Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 10; MXW-AN00 Build/HONORMXW-AN00; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/92.0.4515.105 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = 82400b65-bd87-4883-a1f6-b911150f725b Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ###### Research *Enter an [x] character to confirm the points below:* - [x] I have read the [support page](https://ankidroid.org/docs/help.html) and am reporting a bug or enhancement request specific to AnkiDroid - [x] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [x] I have searched for similar existing issues here and on the user forum - [x] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Based on the AnkiDroidJS API wiki, almost every API that was expected to return data of type int, long, or boolean, is actually returning data of type string. I will push the fix which return correct data types. ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue.  61→

 64→
 65→    /**

 68→     */
 69→
 70→    private val context: Context = activity
 71→
 72→    // Text to speech
 73→    private val talker = JavaScriptTTS()
 74→

 76→    private val speechRecognizer = JavaScriptSTT(context)
 77→
 78→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, boolean: Boolean): ByteArray {
 79→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, boolean.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, int: Int): ByteArray {
 83→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, int.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, long: Long): ByteArray {
 87→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, long.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, string: String): ByteArray {
 91→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, string).toString().toByteArray()
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    /**
 95→     * The method parse json data and return api contract object
 96→     * @param byteArray
 97→     * @return apiContract or null
 98→     */
 99→    private fun parseJsApiContract(byteArray: ByteArray): ApiContract? {
100→        try {
101→            val data = JSONObject(byteArray.decodeToString())
102→            val cardSuppliedApiVersion = data.optString("version", "")
103→            val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact = data.optString("developer", "")
104→            val cardSuppliedData = data.optString("data", "")
105→            val isValid = requireApiVersion(cardSuppliedApiVersion, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
106→            return ApiContract(isValid, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact, cardSuppliedData)
107→        } catch (j: JSONException) {
108→            Timber.w(j)
109→            activity.runOnUiThread {
110→                activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, j.localizedMessage))
111→            }
112→        }
113→        return null
114→    }
115→
116→    /*
117→     * see 02-strings.xml
118→     * Show Error code when mark card or flag card unsupported
119→     * 1 - mark card
120→     * 2 - flag card
121→     *
122→     * show developer contact if js api used in card is deprecated
123→     */
124→    private fun showDeveloperContact(errorCode: Int, apiDevContact: String) {
125→        val errorMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.anki_js_error_code, errorCode)
126→        val snackbarMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.api_version_developer_contact, apiDevContact, errorMsg)
127→
128→        activity.showSnackbar(snackbarMsg, Snackbar.LENGTH_INDEFINITE) {
129→            setMaxLines(3)
130→            setAction(R.string.reviewer_invalid_api_version_visit_documentation) {
131→                activity.openUrl(Uri.parse("https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki"))
132→            }
133→        }
134→    }
135→
136→    /**
137→     * Supplied api version must be equal to current api version to call mark card, toggle flag functions etc.
138→     */
139→    private fun requireApiVersion(apiVer: String, apiDevContact: String): Boolean {
140→        try {
141→            if (apiDevContact.isEmpty() || apiVer.isEmpty()) {
142→                activity.runOnUiThread {
143→                    activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, ""))
144→                }
145→                return false
146→            }
147→            val versionCurrent = Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.sCurrentJsApiVersion)
148→            val versionSupplied = Version.parse(apiVer)
149→
150→            /*
151→            * if api major version equals to supplied major version then return true and also check for minor version and patch version
152→            * show toast for update and contact developer if need updates
153→            * otherwise return false
154→            */
155→            return when {
156→                versionSupplied == versionCurrent -> {
157→                    true
158→                }
159→                versionSupplied.isLowerThan(versionCurrent) -> {
160→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
161→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.update_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
162→                    }
163→                    versionSupplied.isHigherThanOrEquivalentTo(Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.sMinimumJsApiVersion))
164→                }
165→                else -> {
166→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
167→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.valid_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
168→                    }
169→                    false
170→                }
171→            }
172→        } catch (e: Exception) {
173→            Timber.w(e, "requireApiVersion::exception")
174→        }
175→        return false
176→    }
177→
178→    /**
179→     * Handle js api request,
180→     * some of the methods are overridden in Reviewer.kt and default values are returned.
181→     * @param methodName
182→     * @param bytes
183→     * @param returnDefaultValues `true` if default values should be returned (if non-[Reviewer])
184→     * @return
185→     */
186→    open suspend fun handleJsApiRequest(methodName: String, bytes: ByteArray, returnDefaultValues: Boolean = true) = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
187→        // the method will call to set the card supplied data and is valid version for each api request
188→        val apiContract = parseJsApiContract(bytes)!!
189→        // if api not init or is api not called from reviewer then return default -1
190→        // also other action will not be modified
191→        if (!apiContract.isValid or returnDefaultValues) {
192→            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, -1)
193→        }
194→
195→        val cardDataForJsAPI = activity.getCardDataForJsApi()
196→        val apiParams = apiContract.cardSuppliedData
197→
198→        return@withContext when (methodName) {
199→            "init" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
200→            "newCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.newCardCount)
201→            "lrnCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.lrnCardCount)
202→            "revCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.revCardCount)
203→            "eta" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.eta)
204→            "nextTime1" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime1)
205→            "nextTime2" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime2)
206→            "nextTime3" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime3)
207→            "nextTime4" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime4)
208→            "toggleFlag" -> {
209→                if (apiParams !in flagCommands) {
210→                    showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeFlagCard, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
211→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
212→                }
213→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.executeCommand(flagCommands[apiParams]!!))
214→            }
215→            "markCard" -> processAction({ activity.executeCommand(ViewerCommand.MARK) }, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeMarkCard, ::convertToByteArray)
216→            "buryCard" -> processAction(activity::buryCard, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeBuryCard, ::convertToByteArray)
217→            "buryNote" -> processAction(activity::buryNote, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeBuryNote, ::convertToByteArray)
218→            "suspendCard" -> processAction(activity::suspendCard, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendCard, ::convertToByteArray)
219→            "suspendNote" -> processAction(activity::suspendNote, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendNote, ::convertToByteArray)
220→            "setCardDue" -> {
221→                try {
222→                    val days = apiParams.toInt()
223→                    if (days < 0 || days > 9999) {
224→                        showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeSetDue, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
225→                        return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)

227→                    activity.launchCatchingTask {

229→                    }

## Summary

I have successfully fixed the AnkiDroidJS API data type issue. Here's what was done:

### Changes Made

**File: `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt`**

1. **Updated `convertToByteArray` methods (lines 78-92):**
   - Removed `.toString()` calls for Boolean, Int, and Long parameters
   - Now passes native types directly to `ApiResult`

2. **Updated `ApiResult` class (line 428):**
   - Changed value parameter type from `String` to `Any`
   - JSONObject.put() now correctly serializes different types according to JSON standards

### Result

The API now correctly returns:
- **Integers** as JSON numbers: `{"success":true,"value":0}` instead of `{"success":true,"value":"0"}`
- **Longs** as JSON numbers: `{"success":true,"value":1234567890}` instead of `{"success":true,"value":"1234567890"}`
- **Booleans** as JSON booleans: `{"success":true,"value":true}` instead of `{"success":true,"value":"true"}`
- **Strings** remain as JSON strings: `{"success":true,"value":"Test"}` (unchanged)

### Verification

Created comprehensive tests in `/project/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/AnkiDroidJsAPIDataTypeTest.kt` that verify:
- Integer values are returned as numbers
- Long values are returned as numbers  
- Boolean values are returned as booleans
- String values remain as strings
- All tests pass successfully

### JavaScript Impact

In JavaScript, `typeof(response.value)` will now correctly return:
- `"number"` for APIs returning int/long (like `ankiGetCardQueue()`)
- `"boolean"` for APIs returning boolean (like `ankiGetCardMark()`)
- `"string"` for APIs returning string (like `ankiGetDeckName()`)

This fix resolves the issue reported where `ankiGetCardQueue()` was returning `"string"` type instead of the documented `int` type.

365→    private fun processAction(
366→        action: () -> Boolean,
367→        apiContract: ApiContract,
368→        errorCode: Int,
369→        conversion: (ApiContract, Boolean) -> ByteArray
370→    ): ByteArray {
371→        val status = action()
372→        if (!status) {
373→            showDeveloperContact(errorCode, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
374→        }
375→        return conversion(apiContract, status)
376→    }

378→    private suspend fun ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract: ApiContract): ByteArray = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
379→        val cards = try {
380→            searchForCards(apiContract.cardSuppliedData, SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering(), CardsOrNotes.CARDS)
381→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
382→            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript(
383→                "console.log('${context.getString(R.string.search_card_js_api_no_results)}')",
384→                null
385→            )
386→            showDeveloperContact(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeSearchCard, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
387→            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
388→        }
389→        val searchResult: MutableList<String> = ArrayList()
390→        for (s in cards) {
391→            val jsonObject = JSONObject()
392→            val fieldsData = s.card.note(getColUnsafe).fields
393→            val fieldsName = s.card.model(getColUnsafe).fieldsNames
394→
395→            val noteId = s.card.nid
396→            val cardId = s.card.id
397→            jsonObject.put("cardId", cardId)
398→            jsonObject.put("noteId", noteId)
399→
400→            val jsonFieldObject = JSONObject()
401→            fieldsName.zip(fieldsData).forEach { pair ->
402→                jsonFieldObject.put(pair.component1(), pair.component2())
403→            }
404→            jsonObject.put("fieldsData", jsonFieldObject)
405→
406→            searchResult.add(jsonObject.toString())
407→        }
408→
409→        // quote result to prevent JSON injection attack
410→        val jsonEncodedString = JSONObject.quote(searchResult.toString())
411→        activity.runOnUiThread {
412→            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSearchCard($jsonEncodedString)", null)
413→        }
414→        convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
415→    }

432→                put("value", value)
433→            }.toString()

package com.ichi2.anki

import androidx.test.ext.junit.runners.AndroidJUnit4
import org.json.JSONObject
import org.junit.Assert.assertEquals
import org.junit.Assert.assertTrue
import org.junit.Test
import org.junit.runner.RunWith

@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
class AnkiDroidJsAPIDataTypeTest : RobolectricTest() {

    @Test
    fun testIntegerReturnType() = runTest {
        val models = col.notetypes
        val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
        val basic = models.byName(AnkiDroidApp.appResources.getString(R.string.basic_model_name))
        basic!!.put("did", didA)
        addNoteUsingBasicModel("foo", "bar")

        val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
        val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi
        
        waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

        // Test cardQueue returns integer type
        val cardQueueResult = getDataFromRequest("cardQueue", jsapi)
        val jsonObject = JSONObject(cardQueueResult)
        
        assertTrue("Response should have success field", jsonObject.has("success"))
        assertTrue("Response should have value field", jsonObject.has("value"))
        assertTrue("Success should be true", jsonObject.getBoolean("success"))
        
        // Verify the value is an integer, not a string
        val value = jsonObject.get("value")
        assertTrue("cardQueue should return an integer, not a string", value is Int)
    }

    @Test
    fun testLongReturnType() = runTest {
        val models = col.notetypes
        val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
        val basic = models.byName(AnkiDroidApp.appResources.getString(R.string.basic_model_name))
        basic!!.put("did", didA)
        addNoteUsingBasicModel("foo", "bar")

        val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
        val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi
        
        waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

        // Test cardId returns long type
        val cardIdResult = getDataFromRequest("cardId", jsapi)
        val jsonObject = JSONObject(cardIdResult)
        
        assertTrue("Response should have success field", jsonObject.has("success"))
        assertTrue("Response should have value field", jsonObject.has("value"))
        assertTrue("Success should be true", jsonObject.getBoolean("success"))
        
        // Verify the value is a long, not a string
        val value = jsonObject.get("value")
        assertTrue("cardId should return a long, not a string", value is Long || value is Int)
    }

    @Test
    fun testBooleanReturnType() = runTest {
        val models = col.notetypes
        val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
        val basic = models.byName(AnkiDroidApp.appResources.getString(R.string.basic_model_name))
        basic!!.put("did", didA)
        addNoteUsingBasicModel("foo", "bar")

        val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
        val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi
        
        waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

        // Test cardMark returns boolean type
        val cardMarkResult = getDataFromRequest("cardMark", jsapi)
        val jsonObject = JSONObject(cardMarkResult)
        
        assertTrue("Response should have success field", jsonObject.has("success"))
        assertTrue("Response should have value field", jsonObject.has("value"))
        assertTrue("Success should be true", jsonObject.getBoolean("success"))
        
        // Verify the value is a boolean, not a string
        val value = jsonObject.get("value")
        assertTrue("cardMark should return a boolean, not a string", value is Boolean)
    }

    @Test
    fun testAllCardPropertyTypes() = runTest {
        val models = col.notetypes
        val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
        val basic = models.byName(AnkiDroidApp.appResources.getString(R.string.basic_model_name))
        basic!!.put("did", didA)
        addNoteUsingBasicModel("foo", "bar")

        val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
        val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi
        
        waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

        // Test various integer properties
        val intProperties = listOf("cardReps", "cardInterval", "cardFactor", "cardType", "cardLeft", "cardLapses", "cardQueue", "cardDue", "cardFlag")
        for (property in intProperties) {
            val result = getDataFromRequest(property, jsapi)
            val jsonObject = JSONObject(result)
            val value = jsonObject.get("value")
            assertTrue("$property should return a number (int), not a string. Got: ${value::class.java}", value is Int)
        }

        // Test long properties
        val longProperties = listOf("cardId", "cardNid", "cardDid", "cardODid", "cardODue", "cardMod")
        for (property in longProperties) {
            val result = getDataFromRequest(property, jsapi)
            val jsonObject = JSONObject(result)
            val value = jsonObject.get("value")
            assertTrue("$property should return a number (long/int), not a string. Got: ${value::class.java}", value is Long || value is Int)
        }

        // Test boolean properties
        val result = getDataFromRequest("cardMark", jsapi)
        val jsonObject = JSONObject(result)
        val value = jsonObject.get("value")
        assertTrue("cardMark should return a boolean, not a string. Got: ${value::class.java}", value is Boolean)
    }

    @Test
    fun testStringReturnType() = runTest {
        val models = col.notetypes
        val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
        val basic = models.byName(AnkiDroidApp.appResources.getString(R.string.basic_model_name))
        basic!!.put("did", didA)
        addNoteUsingBasicModel("foo", "bar")

        val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
        val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi
        
        waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

        // Test deckName returns string type (should remain as string)
        val deckNameResult = getDataFromRequest("deckName", jsapi)
        val jsonObject = JSONObject(deckNameResult)
        
        assertTrue("Response should have success field", jsonObject.has("success"))
        assertTrue("Response should have value field", jsonObject.has("value"))
        assertTrue("Success should be true", jsonObject.getBoolean("success"))
        
        // Verify the value is a string
        val value = jsonObject.get("value")
        assertTrue("deckName should return a string", value is String)
        assertEquals("Test", value)
    }

    companion object {
        private fun jsApiContract(data: String = ""): ByteArray {
            return JSONObject().apply {
                put("version", "0.0.2")
                put("developer", "test@example.com")
                put("data", data)
            }.toString().toByteArray()
        }

        private suspend fun getDataFromRequest(
            methodName: String,
            jsAPI: AnkiDroidJsAPI,
            apiData: String = ""
        ): String {
            return jsAPI.handleJsApiRequest(methodName, jsApiContract(apiData), false)
                .decodeToString()
        }
    }
}
"}

 35→/**
 36→ * A Card is the ultimate entity subject to review; it encapsulates the scheduling parameters (from which to derive
 37→ * the next interval), the note it is derived from (from which field data is retrieved), its own ownership (which deck it
 38→ * currently belongs to), and the retrieval of presentation elements (filled-in templates).
 39→ *
 40→ * Card presentation has two components: the question (front) side and the answer (back) side. The presentation of the
 41→ * card is derived from the template of the card's Card Type. The Card Type is a component of the Note Type (see Models)
 42→ * that this card is derived from.
 43→ *
 44→ * This class is responsible for:
 45→ * - Storing and retrieving database entries that map to Cards in the Collection
 46→ * - Providing the HTML representation of the Card's question and answer
 47→ * - Recording the results of review (answer chosen, time taken, etc)
 48→ *
 49→ * It does not:
 50→ * - Generate new cards (see Collection)
 51→ * - Store the templates or the style sheet (see Models)
 52→ *
 53→ * Type: 0=new, 1=learning, 2=due
 54→ * Queue: same as above, and:
 55→ * -1=suspended, -2=user buried, -3=sched buried
 56→ * Due is used differently for different queues.
 57→ * - new queue: note id or random int
 58→ * - rev queue: integer day
 59→ * - lrn queue: integer timestamp
 60→ */
 61→open class Card : Cloneable {
 62→
 63→    /**
 64→     * Time in MS when timer was started
 65→     */
 66→    var timerStarted: Long
 67→
 68→    // Not in LibAnki. Record time spent reviewing in MS in order to restore when resuming.
 69→    private var elapsedTime: Long = 0
 70→
 71→    // BEGIN SQL table entries
 72→    @set:VisibleForTesting
 73→    var id: Long
 74→    var nid: NoteId = 0
 75→    var did: DeckId = 0
 76→    var ord = 0
 77→    var mod: Long = 0
 78→    var usn = 0
 79→
 80→    @get:CARD_TYPE
 81→    @CARD_TYPE
 82→    var type = 0
 83→
 84→    @get:CARD_QUEUE
 85→    @CARD_QUEUE
 86→    var queue = 0
 87→    var due: Long = 0
 88→    var ivl = 0
 89→    var factor = 0
 90→    var reps = 0
 91→        private set
 92→    var lapses = 0
 93→    var left = 0
 94→    var oDue: Long = 0
 95→    var oDid: DeckId = 0
 96→    private var customData: String = ""
 97→    private var originalPosition: Int? = null
 98→    private var flags = 0
 99→
100→    // END SQL table entries
